title: Disco: AI Designs Enzymes Nature Never Evolved
summary: Researchers have developed an AI system called Disco that can design enzymes with novel structures and functions. This breakthrough could revolutionize industries like medicine and manufacturing.
body:
Researchers have unveiled Disco, an AI system capable of designing enzymes that do not exist in nature. Unlike traditional methods that rely on natural evolution, Disco uses machine learning to create enzymes with entirely new structures and functions. This innovation could lead to more efficient and sustainable industrial processes.

The ability to design custom enzymes opens up new possibilities for industries such as medicine, agriculture, and manufacturing. For example, enzymes designed to break down specific pollutants could revolutionize waste management, while medical applications could include more effective drug delivery systems. This technology could also reduce the need for trial-and-error experiments, speeding up research and development.

The future of Disco and similar AI-driven enzyme design tools is promising. Researchers are already exploring collaborations with pharmaceutical and biotech companies to bring these designs to market. However, challenges remain, including ensuring the safety and efficacy of these novel enzymes. As AI continues to advance, we can expect even more groundbreaking applications in biotechnology.
twitterThread:
🚀 AI just designed enzymes nature never imagined. Meet Disco. 

Here's why this matters:
Disco creates enzymes with novel structures. Not evolved. Not discovered. Designed from scratch.
The wild part? These enzymes could revolutionize medicine, manufacturing, and waste management.
Let that sink in. AI is now inventing biological tools beyond nature's imagination.
This changes everything for drug delivery, pollution control, and sustainable production.
